---
title: "Setting up a grading pipeline"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Setting up a grading pipeline}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

This vignette walks through a complete grading run end to end: writing tests,
gathering submissions, grading, summarising, and exporting.

## Folder layout

The grader expects one sub-folder per student under a submissions root, and a
flat folder of test files. File names follow a simple convention:

```
submissions/
  garcia_juan/
    ejercicio1.R
    ejercicio2.R
  lopez_maria/
    ejercicio1.R
    ejercicio2.R

tests/
  test_ejercicio1.R
  test_ejercicio2.R
```

A test file holds one or more functions named `test_<exercise>_<case>` that
return `TRUE` or `FALSE`.

```r
# tests/test_ejercicio1.R
test_ejercicio1_roots <- function() {
  all(quadratic_roots(1, 0, -1) == c(-1, 1))
}
test_ejercicio1_numeric <- function() {
  is.numeric(quadratic_roots(1, 0, -1))
}
```

## Running the grader

```r
library(corrector)

results <- grade_submissions("submissions/", test_dir = "tests/")
```

`results` is a `grade_results` object: a long-format data frame with one row
per test, plus the columns `student`, `exercise`, `status`, `message`, and
`duration`. Status is one of `pass`, `fail`, `error`, `timeout`, `missing`, or
`source_error`.

```r
head(as.data.frame(results, format = "long"))
```

Convert to the legacy wide-boolean view with `as.data.frame(results, format =
"wide")`.

## Summarising

`grade_report()` reduces the long results to per-exercise pass rates and
per-student scores, plus counts of errors, timeouts, and missing submissions.

```r
grade_report(results)
```

`plot_report()` produces three plots. Pass `backend = "ggplot2"` to use
ggplot2, if installed.

## Exporting

```r
export_to_csv(results, "grades.csv")               # wide by default
export_to_csv(results, "grades-long.csv", format = "long")
export_to_html(results, "report.html")
```

The HTML report colour-codes each cell by status and includes a per-student
breakdown of every test.

## Timeouts and untrusted code

The default engine runs tests in the current R session via `setTimeLimit()`.
That works for well-behaved student code but cannot interrupt arbitrary busy
loops, and it does not stop a student from touching the file system.

For untrusted code or unreliable timing, pass `engine = "callr"`:

```r
results <- grade_submissions("submissions/", test_dir = "tests/",
                             timeout = 10, engine = "callr")
```

The `callr` engine runs each exercise in a fresh subprocess and applies a
wall-clock kill when the budget is exceeded. Requires the `callr` package.

## Renaming students

The default student-name extractor takes the first underscore-separated
segment and applies title case (so `garcia_juan_12345` becomes `Garcia`).
Pass `student_name_fn` to override:

```r
results <- grade_submissions(
  "submissions/", test_dir = "tests/",
  student_name_fn = function(folder) {
    sub("_.*$", "", folder)
  }
)
```
